We'll forget Pratchett when we forget Tolkien and Lewis and LeGuin and L'Engle and Adams and Asimov.

Which is to say, maybe someday, but not any time soon.

Good AI practice reduces failures. It doesn’t remove them.

The watching is yours.

record.text:
Question 4(d):

AI slop looks like work.

It has headings, structure, confident claims and sometimes even citations.

But underneath, there’s no weight.

This piece shows how to spot it: weak evidence, missing caveats, empty recommendations and rhetorical padding.

www.michellesullivan.org/blog/1921

AI tools aren’t interchangeable.

Some are good at drafting.
Some at analysis.
Some at search.
Some at code.
Some at conversation.

The people getting the best results aren’t loyal to one AI.

They’re building toolkits.

What 49 Vibe-Coded GitHub Projects Revealed About AI Code Duplication

I ran jscpd against 49 vibe-coded GitHub projects looking for code duplication patterns. Average duplication: 7.98% (above the 3-5% industry benchmark). But the unexpected finding wasn't in the apps — it was in…

external.description: I ran jscpd against 49 vibe-coded GitHub projects looking for code duplication patterns. Average duplication: 7.98% (above the 3-5% industry benchmark). But the unexpected finding wasn't in the apps — it was in the skill libraries built to teach AI agents how to code. Those have 30-40% duplication rates, mostly in markdown instructions and CSS templates duplicated for different agent platforms. The infrastructure designed to enforce AI discipline embodies the same pattern it's trying to prevent.
